title: pelma stipulaceum
intro: Pelma stipulaceum, also known as the Ceylon gooseberry or Indian cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant's fruit, leaves, and bark have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pelma stipulaceum include treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
